subject: Computer Networking
title: Insider Threats
description: A short lesson to help you apply Insider Threats.

hook.text: Not all threats come from outside. Sometimes the person with the badge and the password is the danger.

buildup.text: Insider threats include disgruntled employees, careless contractors, or anyone with legitimate access who misuses it.

discovery.text: They bypass perimeter defenses because they're already inside. Firewalls and intrusion detection can't see them.

twist.text: Snowden had authorized NSA access. He downloaded millions of classified files without triggering any alarms.

climax.text: Least-privilege access, activity monitoring, and separation of duties reduce insider risk but can't eliminate it.

punchline.text: The biggest threat might already have the keys.

quiz.question: Why are insider threats difficult to detect?
options: Insiders have legitimate access that bypasses perimeter defenses
Insiders only attack from outside the network
